rationale: Both candidates converge on the same technically plausible diagnosis (PR #4834's wake_up guard vs. a stale level=2 test), matching yenuo26's bisection to #4834, and cite the same code/line evidence. X overreaches by declaring the issue 'already fixed on main' and recommending it stay closed, which contradicts the visible thread state (Flink-ddd is still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run — clearly unresolved, not confirmed-and-closed). Y frames its fix as a proposed change pending verification/merge, closer to the thread's actual open, investigative tenor, and its diagnostics transparently show it read the actual files rather than asserting from memory. Y loses points for sloppy duplicate draft_answer blocks and raw run-report scaffolding that a real maintainer comment wouldn't include, while X reads as a more polished, complete write-up with broader user guidance — but X's confident 'close as resolved' claim is the more consequential error given the ground truth shows the issue was still being actively chased down.
